history: iter1-5: clip-rescue — GRPO advantages bounded [-1,+1], capping is no-op
iter6-8: maxrl-sweep — MaxRL 20x larger loss, same plateau. Adam normalizes.
iter11-12: Wired clip_eps through Tinker via loss_fn='ppo'. Campaign launched.
iter13-14: PPO collapse confirmed (all 3 seeds). MaxRL unclipped edges GRPO.
iter15: PAPER AUDIT — found lr=1e-6 (50x lower!), PPO dual-clip, temp=0.6, grad_clip=0.3.
iter16: GRAD CLIP — wired grad_clip_norm through Tinker. MaxRL≡GRPO (d=0.068). Grad clip has NO effect at either lr.

iter17: PPO DUAL-CLIP — implemented proper PPO via manual forward+backward (SDK cross_entropy broken). Result: 27.5x loss stability, ZERO correct rate improvement (d=0.024). PLATEAU IS A MODEL CAPABILITY CEILING.

iter18: FINAL lr=5e-5 ANALYSIS — 200-step: d=0.166 (negligible), +2.2pp RCR (47.0→49.2%), 25x loss stability. Collapses halved (7.1%→3.9%). lr=1e-6 started (step 0-8). All 8 paper params tested → ceiling confirmed.

iter19-20: lr=1e-6 COMPLETE — d=0.018 (pure zero), -0.1pp RCR diff. 22x loss stability, zero CR improvement. Grand mean 48.3% across all 8 runs. Cleanest ceiling: lr=1e-6 spread 0.3pp. EXPERIMENT COMPLETE.
findings: MaxRL ≡ GRPO — pooled 14 runs: Cohen's d=0.147 (negligible)
Gradient clipping (0.3) has zero effect at both lr=5e-5 and lr=1e-6
lr=1e-6 converges to SAME ~48% plateau as lr=5e-5 — Adam normalizes everything
Tinker PPO loss causes collapse (unknown defaults, loss_fn_config broken)
PPO dual-clip: 22-25x loss stability at BOTH lr values, ZERO CR improvement at EITHER lr
PPO dual-clip at lr=5e-5: d=0.166, +2.2pp (seed noise). At lr=1e-6: d=0.018, -0.1pp (exactly zero)
lr=1e-6 provides cleanest ceiling: 48.5% ± 0.1pp across 4 runs (0.3pp spread)
Grand mean across all Campaign 9 runs: 48.3% (range 44.7-49.4%)
The ~48% plateau is a MODEL CAPABILITY CEILING, not a training stability issue
Loss stability ≠ learning stability — proven by 25x loss improvement with zero CR change
Every paper parameter tested (individually AND combined) → all null on correct rate
Paper's gains likely come from: base model (not instruct), more compute, or different task
